channel.title: C++ on Research Notes
channel.description: Recent content in C++ on Research Notes

item.title: Compiling Native Code
item.description: Visualizing simple C functions compiled with GCC 15.2 into x86-64 assembly to analyze stack frame layouts, register usage, argument passing, and control-flow decisions, all interactively explored through the Compiler Explorer Assignment to Local Variables void func() { int x = 3; } func(): push rbp mov rbp, rsp mov DWORD PTR [rbp-4], 3 nop pop rbp ret push rbp saves the current value of the base pointer (rbp) onto the stack.

item.title: Embedding Shellcode Code Caves
item.description: Theory Load malicious bytes into the “code cave” of a portable executable. This doesn’t change the file size, because you will be replacing bytes that already exist with malicious code. Typically, when a PE file executes, it follows the instructions from top to bottom. In contrary, when a trojan executes, it will immediately jump to the section containing the shellcode, execute the it and then return back the top of the file and run the program as usual.

item.title: Embedding Shellcode in Text, Data and Resource Sections
